task_completion.description: Verify that two tracks named 'Host' and 'Guest' have been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Ardour editor window, look at the track headers on the left side. There should be two audio tracks explicitly labeled 'Host' and 'Guest'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the raw audio files have been imported into the tracks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Audio regions containing waveforms should be visible on the timelines for both the 'Host' and 'Guest' tracks.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Host and Guest tracks are synchronized.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main, thicker waveform blocks (representing the speech) on both tracks should be vertically aligned so they start at the exact same time on the timeline.

task_completion.description: Verify that the pre-roll is trimmed and the conversation starts at timeline position 0.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The left edge of the audio regions for both tracks must align perfectly with the 0:00:00:00 mark on the top timeline ruler. The earlier isolated spikes (sync beeps) should be cut out and no longer visible.

task_completion.description: Verify that a short fade-in has been applied to the start of the tracks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the very beginning (left edge) of the audio regions at position 0. There should be a visible diagonal line or shaded curve indicating a fade-in envelope.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Ardour session has been saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Ardour window title bar should display the session name without an asterisk (*) or 'unsaved' indicator, or a 'Saved' message should be visible in the status bar or undo history.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Ardour GUI to perform the edits.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Ardour application window should be open and show the edited regions. There should be no terminal windows visible where the agent used command-line audio tools (like SoX or FFmpeg) to bypass the DAW.

integrity.description: Verify the audio regions contain genuine waveform data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The tracks should display actual waveform patterns with variable amplitudes (representing speech), not empty regions, flat lines, or solid blocks.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The host beep occurs at 2.0s and the guest beep at 6.5s. The host speech starts at 10.0s and the guest speech at 14.5s. Proper synchronization and trimming means the audio regions should be cut at exactly the 10.0s mark for the Host file and the 14.5s mark for the Guest file, and both of these new start points must be placed exactly at 0:00 on the Ardour timeline.
